rulings.Case Title: NEW YORK CHOCOLATE & CONFECTIONS COMPANY, A VS. OUSMANNE GBANE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Reconsider Order Granting Cross-Defts' Motion To Quash Service Of Summons And Cross-Complaint
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, NOVEMBER 13, 2007, LINE 6, CROSS-COMPLAINANT HOUSMANN BANET'S Motion To Reconsider Order Granting Cross-Defendants' Motion To Quash Service Of Summons And Cross-Complaint IS DENIED. BANET'S MOTION FAILS TO COMPLY WITH CCP 1008. THE DECLARATION IS UNTIMELY AND IT DOES NOT CONTAIN EITHER NEW FACTS OR LAW. FURTHER BANET WAS NOT THE CROSS-COMPLAINANT AND HE CANNOT APPEAR FOR THE LLC UNLESS HE IS AN ATTORNEY AND THERE IS NO EVIDENCE THAT HE IS. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: KAMLESH BANGA et al VS. AMERICAN EXPRESS TRAVEL RELATED SERVICES COMPANY, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Tentative Ruling And Continued Hearing On Deft'S Demurrer And Mtn To Strike Portions Of Plntf'S Second Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, NOVEMBER 13, 2007, LINE 7, DEFENDANT AMERICAN EXPRESS TRAVEL RELATED SERVICES COMPANY, Demurrer And Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiff's Second Amended Complaint. THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED. HEARING REQUIRED TO DETERMINE IF THERE IS ANY BASIS TO AMEND. EACH OF THE CAUSES OF ACTION IS BASED ON THE PRODUCTION OF RECORDS IN RESPONSE TO A SUBPOENA. EVEN IF THE RECORDS WERE PRODUCED IN ERROR, THE DISCLOSURE IS PRIVILEGED UNDER CIVIL CODE SECTION 47(b). THE MOTION TO STRIKE LIKEWISE IS GRANTED AS TO ALL CAUSES OF ACTION. NO PUNITIVE DAMAGES ARE RECOVERABLE UNDER THE 3RD CAUSE OF ACTION. HEARING REQUIRED TO DETERMINE IF THERE IS ANY BASIS TO GRANT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE OTHER CAUSES OF ACTION. =(302/PJM/SN-X)

rulings.Case Title: NANCY CHAIO-CASTLE VS. BOARD OF REGISTERED NURSING DEPARTMENT OF CONSUMER
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandamus And Damages
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, NOVEMBER 13, 2007, LINE 16, PETITIONER NANCY CHIAO-CASTLE'S Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandamus And Damages. THE WRIT IS DENIED. RESPONDENTS OBJECTION TO THE EVIDENCE IN THE AMENDED WRIT IS GRANTED BECAUSE PETITIONER HAS NOT SHOWN ANY BASIS WHY THE EVIDENCE WAS NOT PRESENTED OR COULD NOT BE PRESENTED AT THE ADMINISTRATIVE HEARING. ELIZABETH D. V. ZOLIN (1993) 216 CAL.APP. 4TH 473. IF THE COURT WERE TO CONSIDER SUCH EVIDENCE, IT WOULD NOT PROVIDE A PERSUASIVE BASIS FOR GRANTING THE WRIT. BREAKZONE BILLIARDS V. CITY OF TORRANCE (2001) 81 CAL.APP. 4TH 1205. PETITIONER HAS FAILED TO IDENTIFY EVIDENCE IN THE ADMINISTRATIVE RECORD THAT IS CONTRARY TO OR FAILS TO SUPPORT OR WOULD LEAD THE COURT TO A CONCLUSION CONTRARY TO THE ADMINISTRATIVE DECISION. PETITIONER HAS FAILED TO MEET HER BURDEN OF PROOF. CHAPMAN V. SUPERIOR COURT (2005) 130 CAL. APP. 4TH 261, 271; WARD V. COUNTY OF RIVERSIDE (1969) 273 CAL. APP. 2D 353, 358. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: LORRAINE PARKER
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Hearing & Petition For Releif From Claim Requirement
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, NOVEMBER 13, 2007, LINE 12, PETITIONER LORRAINE PARKER'S Petition For Releif From Claim Requirement IS DENIED. COUNSEL'S DECLARATION IS HEARSAY WITH RESPECT TO THE CRITICAL FACTS THAT FORM THE BASIS FOR THE PETITION. THESE STATEMENTS, IN PARTICULAR PARAGRAPHS 5-7 OF THE HADWEN DECLARATION, ARE NOT ADMISSIBLE. AS A CONSEQUENCE, PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO PRESENT FACTS THAT SUPPORT HER CLAIM. =(302/PJM/PB)
